title: Sidrona
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]]|[[Sidrona Diath]]}} {{Character |type=Galactic Republic |image=[[File:ChancellorSidrona.jpg]] |name=Sidrona |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[3996 BBY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' says that the events of [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 3]] happened in [[3996 BBY]]. [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 3]] |species= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 2]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin=[[Color/Legends|Blue-gray]] |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]{{DB|character|ulicqeldroma|Qel-Droma, Ulic}} |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I just wanted to say that you, and the whole Republic Senate are ''irrelevant''. The Jedi Knights have coddled you for centuries. You are outdated and ineffective…puppets to tradition…|Exar Kun, to Sidrona|}} '''Sidrona''' was a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. He was a firm supporter of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], and a friend of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] and [[Minister of Defense (Republic)|Minister of Defense]] [[Netus]]. In [[3996 BBY]], the Republic fell prey to a group of unidentified [[Pirate/Legends|terrorists]], who plundered and looted many [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] for a number of months, avoiding detection and apprehension by the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]]. Rumors abounded that a rogue [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] was responsible, though Sidrona dismissed them; he and the Senate refused the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] clearance to intervene and deal with the supposed rogue Jedi itself. The Order sent Vodo-Siosk Baas to meet with Sidrona and the Senate and persuade them to overturn their decision, though as they rendezvoused, [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]]—a former Jedi, who had been responsible for the terrorist strikes—launched an [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Sith War)|attack]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], capital world of the Republic. The Republic was able to fend his forces off, though, and captured Qel-Droma; the Chancellor decreed that he would be put on trial, held accountable for his crimes, and punished with [[Death/Legends|death]]. Qel-Droma refused to show regret for his actions at [[Trial of Ulic Qel-Droma|the trial]], but before Sidrona and his fellow Senate members could order his execution, Qel-Droma's master, [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] forced entry into the [[Senate Hall]]. He used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] [[Qâzoi Kyantuska|to subdue]] the audience and approached the pulpit on which Sidrona stood. Sidrona attempted to attack Kun with his staff; the Sith Lord was too powerful, though, using the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] to force Sidrona into telling the congregation that the Senate was a meaningless institution. With that, Kun killed the Chancellor. ==Biography== ===Terrorist strikes=== {{Quote|Frankly, I think your concern is premature, Vodo. So far, all we have is a ''rumor'' of Jedi involvement—nothing concrete.|Sidrona speaks to Vodo-Siosk Baas on Coruscant|}} Sidrona was a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], slightly shorter than an average [[Human/Legends|Human]], with gray-blue skin. He had a bulky body, with a large head that had tentacles over his mouth. He lived at the height of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] more than [[4000 BBY/Legends|four thousand]] years before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. A powerful public speaker, Sidrona became a [[Senator/Legends|senator]] of the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] eventually became the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]], though was also given the title "[[President of the Senate]]."''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' He was a friend of the Republic's [[Minister of Defense (Republic)|Minister of Defense]], [[Netus]], as well as [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]]. His reign coincided with a series of terrorist raids on Republic [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]]. An unknown force was striking at various ship construction facilities throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], pillaging the half-completed vessels and leaving few survivors. The Republic was reeling from the attacks, and the Senate authorized a task force led by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Orley Vanicus]] to attempt to intercept the [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]]' next raid and put a stop to them. Rumors abounded that a rogue member of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] was orchestrating and leading the attacks, but Sidrona refused to acknowledge that, as he was a supporter of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Sidrona hoped that Vanicus and his fleet would be able to stop the terrorists, and the Senate refused the Jedi Order permission to intervene and deal with the supposed rogue Jedi themselves. The Jedi Masters, however, were concerned by the rumors, believing that they had substance, and Vodo-Siosk Baas was dispatched to attempt to persuade the Senate to overturn its decision and allow the Jedi to take action. [[File:VodoAndSidrona.jpg|thumb|left|230px|Vodo meets with Sidrona.]] Sidrona and the Minister of Defense, Netus, greeted Baas as he landed [[Vodo-Siosk Baas's courier|his ship]] and disembarked along with Jedi Knights [[Dace Diath]], [[Shoaneb Culu]], [[Qrrrl Toq]], [[Cay Qel-Droma]], [[Nomi Sunrider]], and [[Tott Doneeta]]. Baas wished they were meeting under better circumstances, though Sidrona was quick to point out that he felt the Jedi Masters' concern was premature, and that no solid evidence had been provided to support the rumors of Jedi involvement; Baas, however, stated that he had reason to believe it was more than just a rumor. Their conversation was interrupted by the arrival of one of Netus's staff, who relayed a message sent by Admiral Orley Vanicus: the leader of the terrorist strikes was [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]], a Jedi Knight, and he was preparing a strike against the [[Space station/Legends|space station]] at [[Kemplex Nine]]; Vanicus had sent the majority of the Republic Navy there to intercept Qel-Droma. The strike at Kemplex Nine proved to be a ruse, engineered by Ulic Qel-Droma to move Republic forces away from his true target—Coruscant, the galactic capital. Vanicus had played directly into his hands. Baas's Jedi escorts sensed the impending attack through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], but there was no time to prepare: Qel-Droma's forces arrived from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and started laying siege to Coruscant immediately. Sidrona and the Jedi began to flee the landing pad—which had been targeted by Qel-Droma's raiders, who destroyed Baas's ship—while Netus ordered his staff to muster up whatever defenses they could. The party of ten, consisting of Sidrona, Netus, and the Jedi, was ushered to safety by Republic soldiers, though carnage and battle followed them wherever they went. [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droids]] rained down on the planet's streets, but the Jedi were able to stop them from harming Sidrona and those accompanying him. Baas ordered Netus to find them a way to the Republic [[Command center|war room]], where he believed the battle would be decided. ===The trial of Ulic Qel-Droma=== {{Quote|Ulic Qel-Droma, you are a threat to the peace and safety of the Galactic Republic. Your list is of crimes is long—the destruction of shipyards, the looting of supply bases, the enslavement of mercenaries. And, even worse—you have betrayed your training as a Jedi—your vow to protect the Republic! How do you plead?''"
"''I don't plead with fools. You are puppets of tradition pretending to be important. The coming golden age has no place for you. Your Republic is an empty, self-indulgent diversion…signifying ''nothing''. The lost glory of the Sith will turn all of your accomplishments to dust!|Sidrona and Ulic Qel-Droma|}} [[File:SidronaTalksToUlic.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Sidrona announces Qel-Droma's trial.]] The group eventually made use of a [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicle which had space for all the Jedi as well as Sidrona and the Minister of Defense, though their passage to the war room was impeded by a number of Mandalorian foot soldiers. The Jedi were able to cut through them and sensed that Qel-Droma himself was present at the war room. As they neared it, however, the Mandalorian and [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] forces pulled back and retreated entirely, leaving Qel-Droma unprotected in the war room—Sidrona hoped that the Jedi could apprehend him, so that the Senate could put him on trial to make an example of him. They reached the war room, but in the end it never came to direct confrontation, as the Jedi used the Force to halt Qel-Droma. Sidrona thanked Baas for capturing the rogue Jedi; he decreed that Qel-Droma would stand trial and confidently stated that he would be given the death sentence. Qel-Droma was imprisoned and the Senate arranged a [[Trial of Ulic Qel-Droma|trial]] post-haste, conducted at the enormous [[Senate Hall]] on Coruscant. The senators gathered, baying for Qel-Droma's [[Blood/Legends|blood]], and Qel-Droma was put on the stand, shackled; Sidrona would act as the prosecution and key speaker. He made a short speech outlining the accused's crimes, branding him a traitor, and lambasting the young [[Human/Legends|Human]] for turning his back on the Jedi Order and its [[Jedi Code/Legends|code]], before asking how he would plea. Qel-Droma, however, was unrepentant: he refused to plead with the Senate, claiming that they were fools and puppets of tradition, and that his [[Sith Empire (Exar Kun)/Legends|Sith Empire]] would crush their accomplishments and abolish the Senate. As Qel-Droma defamed the senators and the Chancellor, his brother, the Jedi Knight Cay Qel-Droma, ran toward the pulpit. He pleaded with his brother to be silent and allow him to try to persuade the senators of Ulic's innocence, but the former Jedi refused. Cay attempted anyway, telling the Chancellor and his colleagues that Ulic had done much good for the Republic, fighting in the [[Beast Wars]] of [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] and the [[Freedon Nadd Uprising]], but that he had been traumatized by the death of his Jedi Master, [[Arca Jeth]]. The senators still wanted Qel-Droma dead, though, and the man standing trial himself ordered his brother to stop trying to help him. As the Qel-Dromas argued, however, the Senate Hall's doors were forced open by another being, [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]], Qel-Droma's master. ===Kun's intervention=== {{Quote|Go on, show us!''"
"''We are puppets…irrelevant.''"
"''The Sith Empire will rise again, and we are the ''spark''.''"
"''…we are the spark.''"
"''The flames will wash over the crumbling Republic and consume all your worlds…establishing the glorious days of a long lost golden age, far greater than any of us can know. You can choose either to be part of this great rebirth, or you can stand in our way.|Exar Kun and Sidrona|}} Kun strode into the chamber with confidence, flanked by a number of his [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] warrior [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]] and Mandalorian [[Crusader (Victory-class)|Crusaders]], including [[Mandalore the Indomitable]]. He [[Qâzoi Kyantuska|used the Force]] to prevent the senators from speaking or moving, though Sidrona was unaffected by the Sith spell. The Jedi, also unaffected by Kun's powers, engaged his warriors in combat, though Kun's true target was the Chancellor. He ordered Qel-Droma and Mandalore the Indomitable to occupy the Jedi, while he strode toward Sidrona's pulpit. He ascended the steps until he was on the same level as the Chancellor, who pleaded for the Senate Hall's security guards to come to his aid; everyone in the chamber, however, was incapacitated by Kun's sorcery. [[File:KunConfrontsSidrona.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Sidrona vainly attempts to stop Exar Kun.]] Sidrona attempted to defend himself, swinging his metal staff at Kun, though the Dark Lord of the Sith easily blocked the strike with his [[Sith talisman/Legends|Sith amulet]], sending the staff sprawling out of the Chancellor's hands. Kun then attacked him, grabbing his head and using the power of his Sith amulet to force Sidrona to repeat Kun's anti-Senate, anti-Republic rhetoric. In tremendous pain, and with fluid rapidly oozing from his head, the Chancellor told the congregation that the Senate was outdated, useless, and that it would be of no consequence during the impending golden age of the Sith. During that display, Kun buried his fingers into the back of the Supreme Chancellor's skull and into his brain, before releasing his grip on Sidrona, who was by then dead, and throwing him at the ground. Kun left Sidrona's body on the pulpit, though Master Vodo-Siosk Baas arrived just moments later. He engaged Kun in a [[Duel in the Senate|duel]], though the Dark Lord of the Sith eventually prevailed and slew the [[Krevaaki]] Jedi. Kun and Qel-Droma subsequently denounced the Senate and the Republic, claiming that the [[Brotherhood of the Sith]] controlled the galaxy. Ultimately, Kun was vanquished and by the time of the [[Mandalorian Wars]] forty years after the demise of Sidrona, [[Tol Cressa|he had been replaced]].[[Knights of the Old Republic 12|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 12]] ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|It's time for us to leave here. We've made our point. The Senate members may think they are running things, but they are only acting in a shadow play. We are tired of serving their weakness, and we will work toward our own ends from now on. The Sith Brotherhood controls the galaxy.|Exar Kun, shortly after the Chancellor's death|}} [[File:TrustMe-ItsBrainMatter-TotJTSW3.jpg|thumb|right|240px|The Chancellor's last moments.]] Sidrona was a staunch supporter of the Jedi. During the early stages of the Great Sith War, he refused to believe that a lone, rogue Jedi Knight was leading the attacks on the Republic. He and his fellow Senate members stopped the Jedi from intervening until there was hard evidence of Jedi involvement. Sidrona was a powerful and charismatic public speaker, and not one for small talk. When Baas met with Sidrona and Netus on Coruscant, he cut straight to the chase and spoke with Vodo about the persisting rumors of the rogue Jedi leading attacks on the Republic. He also proved himself a strong orator when Ulic Qel-Droma was captured, and in his subsequent trial. However, Exar Kun was able to break down his resolve and control him, ultimately causing Sidrona's death. ==Behind the scenes== Sidrona was created by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] for [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War|''The Sith War'']], part of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] comic series. He appeared in issues [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|two]] and [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 3|three]] of ''The Sith War''. Anderson and [[Daniel Wallace]] mentioned the character in [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']], referring to him simply as the "President of the Senate." However, Ulic Qel-Droma's [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry calls him "Supreme Chancellor." Sidrona was first named in [[The Horse's Mouth|''Straight from the Horse's Mouth: A Guide to the Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi Universe, Part 3'']], an article in [[Star Wars Insider 28|''Star Wars Insider'' 28]] in [[1995]]. There was initially some confusion over the identity of the character, who is often erroneously believed to be Netus, a separate character.{{JCFCite|url=posts/22633392|board=Literature|thread=Netus as Chancellor|user=Ive_Got_Two_Legs|date=2005-12-16|time=2:09 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801145748/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/netus-as-chancellor.22633392}}. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Sidrona}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 2]] {{1st}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 3]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 4]] {{C|Mentioned in opening crawl only}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{C|Mentioned in Codex entry}} *[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']] {{Ab|Darth Plagueis (audiobook)}} {{Imo}} ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|28|The Horse's Mouth|Straight from the Horse's Mouth: A Guide to the ''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'' Universe, Part 3}} {{1stID|Sidrona}} *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *{{DB|character|ulicqeldroma|Qel-Droma, Ulic}} *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Updated and Expanded]]'' {{Imo}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Chancellor}} {{Interlang |es=Sidrona |fi=Sidrona |fr=Sidrona |esFA=1 }} [[Category:Galactic Republic senators]] [[Category:Individuals of unidentified species]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Supreme Chancellors]]
